tldr: Replace Convert's SaaS feature flagging with GO Feature Flag, a self-hosted, OpenFeature-native MIT solution with 15+ language SDKs.
why_switch: Free, MIT-licensed alternative to Convert's monthly subscription
15+ language SDKs via the relay proxy for polyglot stacks
Flag definitions live in Git, S3, or HTTP for GitOps workflows
Local evaluation removes per-call remote latency
what_to_export: Rollout percentages and gradual rollout schedules
Default values and fallbacks per flag
Stale flags to retire during migration
A/B experiment definitions to handle separately
export_method: Use Convert's dashboard or API (consult official Convert docs for the current export endpoint) to retrieve flags, variants, and targeting rules. If a bulk export is unavailable, script their REST API or transcribe definitions. Convert each flag into the GO Feature Flag YAML/JSON schema described in the official gofeatureflag.org docs.
import_method: Store flag definitions in Git (or S3/HTTP) using the GO Feature Flag schema. Run the GO Feature Flag relay proxy and point it at that source. In each service, install the OpenFeature SDK for that language plus the GO Feature Flag provider, then call OpenFeature's getBooleanValue / getStringValue / getObjectValue for evaluation. For Go services, you can embed the library directly without the relay proxy.

concept_mapping.note: Defined in YAML/JSON, stored in Git/S3/HTTP
Use OpenFeature SDK with the GO Feature Flag provider
Primary workflow is GitOps; consult docs for any UI options
Separate config files or branches per environment

gotchas: You own the operational story: relay proxy deployment, scaling, HA
UI is less polished than SaaS; non-engineers may need a separate workflow
Experimentation analytics are basic; pair with a dedicated analytics tool if needed
At very high QPS, plan SDK caching / relay proxy scaling carefully
Keep OpenFeature SDK and GO Feature Flag provider versions aligned to avoid evaluation drift

email.subject: Moving feature flags from Convert to GO Feature Flag
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving feature flagging from Convert to GO Feature Flag so we get a self-hosted, OpenFeature-native setup at zero subscription cost. Plan: export Convert's flags and targeting, encode them in GO Feature Flag's YAML schema in Git, run the relay proxy, and swap services to the OpenFeature SDK plus the GO Feature Flag provider. Polyglot services pick up the same flags via the relay. We'll handle experimentation analytics separately. Effort estimate: 2-5 days.

Thanks
